content: Just as human linguistic reflection (taught as a core critical thinking skill in high English language curricula, the original lexical origin of the term reflexion) requires holding multiple inconsistent interpretations of a text to deepen understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by explicitly borrowing this structured language learning practice. By asking LLMs to first draft multiple conflicting readings of their own previous outputs the same way students analyze competing perspectives in a reading club, we can build more robust self-correction that aligns with how humans naturally practice reflective thinking.
